# PPTX fixtures

`minimal.pptx` is generated by `scripts/generate-minimal-fixture.mjs` using
PptxGenJS 4.0.1 (MIT). Its text and layout were authored for this repository,
so the fixture contains no third-party presentation content.

Run `npm run fixtures` to copy the committed fixture into the e2e test Vault.
Run `npm run fixtures:regenerate` only when intentionally rebuilding it; the
ZIP container records generation metadata, so review and commit the new binary.

The `failure/` corpus is also repository-authored. It contains intentionally
truncated ZIP, malformed XML, missing-media, encrypted, inert active-content,
candidate/preflight resource-limit, safe standard external-hyperlink, and blocked
external-image inputs, including a relationship-type spoof case. The protected
fixture is a real ECMA-376 Agile-encrypted form of repository-authored test
content, generated with msoffcrypto-tool 6.0.0 (MIT) and the non-secret password
`repository-fixture-password`. Run `npm run fixtures:failure` to copy the
committed bytes into the test Vault. To regenerate intentionally, install
`scripts/requirements-fixtures.txt`, set `MSOFFCRYPTO_PYTHON` if needed, and run
`npm run fixtures:failure:regenerate`.

The `compatibility/` corpus is repository-authored and generated by
`scripts/generate-compatibility-fixtures.mjs` with PptxGenJS 4.0.1 and JSZip
3.10.1 (both MIT). It covers text, fonts, themes and masters, 16:9 and 4:3
layouts, images, transparency, shapes, tables, charts, native DrawingML
grouping, rotation, and complex vector drawings. Run
`npm run fixtures:compatibility` for the stable copy step and
`npm run fixtures:compatibility:regenerate` only to intentionally rebuild it.
